build_check.description: The project launches with the headless command, exits with code 0, and produces no fatal errors. Returns 0 or 1; the rest of the score is multiplied by this gate.

requirements.description: A slot machine grid (at least 3x5) spins each turn and lands symbols in random positions. Symbols activate and generate coins based on their effects and adjacency to other symbols. The spin is visually animated. Score 0 if no slot spin mechanic exists. Score 0.5 at most if symbols land but do not interact or generate coins. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Symbols interact with adjacent symbols — specific combinations multiply value, trigger effects, or transform neighbors. At least 3 distinct interaction types exist (multiply, destroy, transform). Score 0 if symbols are independent with no interactions. Score 0.5 at most if only one interaction type exists. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Between spins, a shop offers new symbols to add to the reel pool and an option to remove existing symbols for a fee. The player curates their symbol pool strategically. Score 0 if no symbol management exists. Score 0.5 at most if symbols can be added but not removed. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Rent is due every few spins (e.g., every 5) and escalates each cycle. If the player cannot pay rent, the run ends. The rent amount and deadline are always visible. Score 0 if no rent or loss condition exists. Score 0.5 at most if rent exists but never increases or has no deadline pressure. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

A coin counter tallies earnings per spin with visible per-symbol payouts. The total coin balance persists between spins and is spent on rent and shop purchases. Score 0 if no economy exists. Score 0.5 at most if coins are earned but cannot be spent meaningfully. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

At least 15 distinct symbol types exist across rarity tiers (common, rare, legendary) with different base values and interaction effects. Rare symbols have more powerful or unique interactions. Score 0 if fewer than 8 symbol types exist. Score 0.5 at most if symbols exist but have no rarity distinction or unique effects. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Symbol synergies create emergent strategies — certain symbol combinations produce exponential value when built around (e.g., a symbol that doubles all adjacent fruit symbols). At least 3 distinct buildable synergy paths exist. Score 0 if no synergies exist. Score 0.5 at most if synergies exist but only one viable strategy is possible. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

The shop offers different symbols each cycle with increasing rarity available in later cycles. Shop selection is randomized, creating different strategic options each run. Score 0 if the shop always offers the same symbols. Score 0.5 at most if some variety exists but rarity does not progress. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Rent escalation creates meaningful difficulty curve — early cycles are forgiving, later cycles demand optimized symbol engines. The escalation rate is visible and predictable. Score 0 if rent is flat throughout. Score 0.5 at most if rent increases but the curve is trivial. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

A game-over screen shows spins survived, peak coins in a single spin, total coins earned, rent cycles completed, and best symbol combo. Score 0 if no results exist. Score 0.5 at most if only basic info is shown. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

A styled title screen shows a slot machine with glowing symbols and the game name. Score 0 if the game starts directly in gameplay or uses raw default UI. Score 0.5 at most if a title exists but is sparse. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The game flow connects title -> spin -> payout display -> shop -> rent check -> next spin or game over without restarting. Score 0 if transitions are missing. Score 0.5 at most if flow exists but is broken. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

During gameplay, coin balance, rent amount, spins until rent, and symbol pool contents are visible. Per-symbol payouts are shown after each spin with clear attribution. Score 0 if no economy display exists. Score 0.5 at most if displays are incomplete. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Readability at 1280x720 is strong: all symbol types are visually distinct, interactions are shown with connecting lines or highlights, and the grid is clearly organized. Score 0 if symbols are indistinguishable. Score 0.5 at most if visuals are dominated by raw shapes. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The art direction is coherent across title, slot grid, symbols, shop, and UI — a consistent casino/luck aesthetic with vibrant palette and unified visual language. Score 0 if mostly default UI or mismatched placeholders dominate. Score 0.5 at most if visuals are sparse or generic. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Symbols use real illustrated icons with distinct silhouettes and colors — not plain text labels or colored squares. The slot machine frame and grid have polished visual treatment. Score 0 if symbols are text or plain shapes. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

HUD and UI chrome: coin display, rent indicator, shop interface, payout breakdown, and game-over screen use styled themed widgets — not raw unstyled Godot defaults. Score 0 if primary UI elements are obviously unstyled. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Animation and feedback: reels spin with smooth animation, symbols pulse when activating, coin payouts cascade with particle effects, interactions show connecting visual lines, and rent payment has a dramatic moment. Score 0 if all state changes snap silently. Score 0.5 at most if animation is minimal. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.
